---
title: "Project 2 Report: Interactive Data Wrangling Studio"
author: "Team Member 1, Team Member 2, Team Member 3"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: false
    number_sections: false
---

## Project Overview

It is designed to support operations such as data uploading, cleaning, preprocessing, feature engineering, exploratory data analysis, and data export. The objective of this application is to provide a user-friendly workflow that enables users to complete common preliminary data science tasks, thereby avoiding the lengthy preparatory work typically required at the onset of model building and analysis.

## App Functionalities

The application contains several tabs that guide the user through the full workflow. The **Load Data** tab allows users to upload data files in CSV, Excel, JSON, and RDS formats, or choose a built-in dataset for testing. After loading a dataset, it shows a preview and a short summary of the data source.

The **Cleaning** tab supports standardizes the data, identifies duplicate records, and handles missing values using various methods—such as retaining missing values, removing rows, or imputing numerical values. Furthermore, the application displays an overview of the missing values to help users fully understand the data quality status before proceeding.

The **Preprocessing**  tab provides functionalities for outlier handling, numerical scaling, and categorical encoding. Users can choose to truncate or remove outliers; apply standard scaling, Min-Max scaling, or robust scaling to numerical columns; and perform label encoding or one-hot encoding on categorical variables.

The **Feature Engineering**  tab allows users to create new variables, encompassing operations such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, logarithms, and square transformations. The application saves these feature processing recipes and instantly displays the impact of these new variables within the dataset preview and feature distribution charts.

The **EDA / Visualization** tab provides visualizations, summary statistics, filtering tools, and correlation heatmaps. Users can freely select chart types, dynamically choose variables, and filter data based on numerical ranges or categorical hierarchies.

Finally, the **Export / Download** tab allows users to download the transformed dataset as a CSV file.

## How to Use the App

Users first launch the application, where they can choose to utilize a built-in dataset or upload a custom file. Once the data has finished loading, users can proceed sequentially through steps such as data cleaning, preprocessing, and feature engineering. At each stage, the application automatically updates the current dataset, allowing users to monitor the status of the data via the "Preview" panel. Users can visualize the processed data using the "EDA" tab, while the final dataset can be downloaded from the "Export" tab.

The recommended workflow is:

1. click "Load Data" section to load/upload a dataset.
2. click "Cleaning" to apply options for duplicates and missing values.
3. click "Preprocessing" to select preprocessing methods for outliers, scaling, and encoding.
4. click " Feature Engineering" to create one or more engineered features.
5. click "EDA/Visualization" to explore plots, summary statistics, and correlations.
6. click "Export/Download" to download the final processed dataset in csv.

## Deployment

The application was deployed online using shinyapps.io.

**Deployed application link:**  
[https://5243project.shinyapps.io/project2-data-wrangling/](https://5243project.shinyapps.io/project2-data-wrangling/)


## Contributions

- **Jason Zhao:** Designed the app structure, implemented the Shiny user interface, and organized the multi-tab workflow.
- **Team Member 2:** Developed the data cleaning, preprocessing, and feature engineering logic.
- **Team Member 3:** Implemented the EDA visualizations, deployment steps, and project documentation.


## Conclusion

In summary, this project produced a complete Shiny application for interactive data wrangling and exploration. The app integrates data upload, cleaning, preprocessing, feature engineering, visualization, and export in a single workflow. Its deployment on shinyapps.io also makes it easy to demonstrate and evaluate as a web-based data application.
